ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists for this specific Singaporean bundle meal. FOODON_00002473 (mixed dish food product) is the closest defensible broader parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP (composite meal) is the closest defensible code for a multi-component bundle meal comprising noodles, fried rice, and fried chicken. No exact FoodEx2 code exists for this specific Singaporean dish combination.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this specific Singaporean bundle meal composite dish.

serving.description: Complete set meal for 4 people (approximately 625g per person)
serving.natural_language_portion: One realistic serving for a single diner from this bundle is approximately 625g, comprising half a portion of white bee hoon (~200g), half a portion of egg fried rice (~200g), and a share of prawn paste chicken (~225g).

nutrient_source.title: Health Promotion Board Singapore — Nutrient Composition of Food (HPB Foodo / Energy and Nutrient Composition of Food)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single HPB entry exists for this specific bundle meal. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted composition of three HPB-sourced component dishes: white bee hoon (HPB), egg fried rice (HPB), and har cheong gai / prawn paste fried chicken (HPB). The input record's per_100g values (energy 192 kcal, protein 11.2g, fat 9.6g, carbohydrate 15.2g) are consistent with this weighted blend and are retained with medium confidence. USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy used as secondary cross-check for individual ingredient macros.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; University of Sydney GI Database — white rice and rice vermicelli entries
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific bundle meal. GI estimated as weighted average of primary carbohydrate components: white rice (GI ~72, University of Sydney GI Database) and rice vermicelli / bee hoon (GI ~58–61, University of Sydney GI Database). The bundle is approximately 50% rice-based and 50% bee hoon-based by carbohydrate contribution, yielding a blended GI of approximately 72 (rounded to the higher rice GI given the egg fried rice component uses white rice). Glycemic load per 625g serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (72 × 95g) / 100 ≈ 68; per-person serving GL ≈ 34.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 320mg/100g from prawn paste (har cheong), oyster sauce, soy sauce, and fish cake; a full per-person serving (~625g) delivers approximately 2000mg sodium, approaching or exceeding the 2000mg daily limit recommended by HPB Singapore for hypertensive individuals.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to no more than 300g of this bundle meal per sitting, request reduced prawn paste marinade and soy/oyster sauce quantities, and substitute fish cake with fresh seafood to meaningfully reduce sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High-GI refined carbohydrates (white rice GI ~72, rice vermicelli GI ~58–61) with low dietary fibre (0.8g/100g) cause rapid postprandial glucose elevation; a per-person serving delivers approximately 95g carbohydrate and a glycemic load of ~34.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with type 2 diabetes should substitute white rice with brown rice and white bee hoon with whole-grain or lower-GI noodles, increase vegetable portions to add fibre, and limit the per-person serving to no more than 300g to moderate glycaemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 3.2g/100g from deep-frying oil and prawn paste marinade, combined with cholesterol 48mg/100g and sodium 320mg/100g, presents a moderate cardiovascular risk; trans fat is low at 0.06g/100g.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should request grilled or steamed chicken instead of deep-fried har cheong gai to reduce saturated fat and AGE formation, limit portion to 300g per sitting to keep saturated fat intake within the 20g daily limit, and choose reduced-sodium condiment options.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying the prawn paste chicken generates advanced glycation end products (AGEs) that promote joint inflammation; sodium 320mg/100g from prawn paste, oyster sauce, and soy sauce further approaches RA dietary thresholds.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should request grilled or steamed chicken to eliminate AGE formation, increase anti-inflammatory vegetables (choy sum, bean sprouts) and aromatics (ginger, garlic), and substitute fish cake with fresh seafood to reduce sodium.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Bundle Meal for 4 with Prawn Paste Chicken, White Bee Hoon and Egg Fried Rice is a Singaporean Chinese sharing meal designed for four diners. It comprises two portions of white bee hoon (stir-fried rice vermicelli with seafood, fish cake, bean sprouts, choy sum, and umami-rich oyster and soy sauce), two portions of egg fried rice (wok-tossed white rice with egg, garlic, and light seasoning), and a complimentary serving of har cheong gai — deep-fried chicken marinated in fermented prawn paste (har cheong), which imparts a pungent, savoury, and slightly funky flavour with a crispy golden exterior. The bee hoon is silky and light with a mild seafood sweetness; the fried rice is fluffy and aromatic; the prawn paste chicken is intensely flavoured, crispy, and rich. This bundle is a popular hawker-style sharing format in Singapore, commonly ordered for family meals or group dining. Nutritionally, per 100g the meal provides 192 kcal, 11.2g protein, 9.6g fat (3.2g saturated), 15.2g carbohydrate, 0.8g dietary fibre, and 320mg sodium. The high sodium content from prawn paste, oyster sauce, soy sauce, and fish cake makes this meal a concern for hypertensive individuals. The refined carbohydrate base (white rice GI ~72, rice vermicelli GI ~58–61) with low fibre results in a high glycaemic index (~72) and a high glycaemic load per serving (~34), making it unsuitable for individuals with type 2 diabetes without modification. The deep-frying of the chicken generates AGEs, raising concern for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis and cardiovascular disease. Allergens include crustaceans (prawn paste), egg, gluten (soy sauce, oyster sauce), and fish (fish cake). The dish is not halal-certified as prawn paste may contain non-halal processing agents depending on the brand.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for this specific Singaporean bundle meal. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted composition of HPB Singapore component dish entries (white bee hoon, egg fried rice, har cheong gai) and cross-checked against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for individual ingredients. The input record's per_100g values are consistent with this estimation and are retained. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database entries for white rice and rice vermicelli. Medium confidence is appropriate for a composite estimated record.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite Singaporean bundle meal is not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as of 2024. potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: micronutrient and moisture data for this specific composite bundle meal are not reported in the HPB Singapore database or USDA FoodData Central for this dish combination as of 2024. trans_fat_g is reported as 0.06g/100g from the input record; this low value is plausible for deep-fried chicken and is retained. The ethnic_tags in the input ('BBQ', 'Crab') appear to be data entry errors for this record and have been excluded as they do not describe this dish. The input source URL (https://foodo.sg/) has been corrected to the authoritative HPB source URL. Halal status is uncertain as commercial prawn paste brands vary in halal certification; is_halal is set to false pending verification. All core macros (protein_g 11.2g, fat_g 9.6g, carbohydrate_g 15.2g) are non-zero and require no zero-macro justification. Iteration 3 fix: ontology.foodon_match_type corrected from 'approximate' to 'closest_parent' and ontology.foodex2_match_type corrected from 'approximate' to 'closest_parent' to conform to the allowed enumeration values ['closest_parent', 'exact', 'none'] as required by g5_ontology_honesty.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Corrected food_name from promotional bundle title to canonical descriptive English name.
Iteration 1: Added all four mandatory Singaporean local-language names (Chinese simplified/traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi).
Iteration 1: Replaced forbidden GrabFood source with HPB Singapore as primary nutrient source with USDA cross-check.
Iteration 1: Added GI estimate (72, high) from University of Sydney GI Database analogous entries for white rice and rice vermicelli.
Iteration 1: Added all three required health_context conditions (Hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, Cardiovascular Disease) with clinical specificity.
Iteration 1: Added defensible ontology anchors (FoodOn FOODON_00002473, FoodEx2 A0CKP) with gap notes.
Iteration 1: Expanded natural_language_description to over 200 characters with full clinical and cultural context.
Iteration 1: Populated aliases_by_language and verified all entries appear verbatim in flat aliases array.
Iteration 1: Documented all -1 fields in notes with source and reason.
Iteration 1: Removed erroneous ethnic_tags (BBQ, Crab) from cultural context.
Iteration 2: Attempted to correct ontology match_type values to 'approximate' — this value was not in the allowed enumeration and caused g5 rejection.

Iteration 3: Corrected ontology.foodon_match_type from 'approximate' to 'closest_parent' and ontology.foodex2_match_type from 'approximate' to 'closest_parent', conforming to the allowed enumeration ['closest_parent', 'exact', 'none']. This resolves the g5_ontology_honesty hard gate failure identified by the validator.
